title: Travel & Accommodation
titleNative: りょこうとしゅくはく
shortSummary: Check in, ask about your room, and handle hotel and ryokan situations while travelling.
culturalNote: Japan offers two very different lodging styles: modern hotels and the traditional ryokan, an inn with tatami floors, futon bedding, and often an onsen (hot spring bath). At a ryokan you remove your shoes at the entrance, wear the provided yukata robe, and may be served an elaborate multi-course dinner in your room. Check-in is efficient and polite, and staff bow deeply on arrival and departure. Bathing etiquette in shared baths is strict: you wash thoroughly before entering the communal water, and bathing suits are not worn. Many onsen still do not permit visible tattoos, so check ahead. Tipping is not expected anywhere, and you will be looked after with omotenashi hospitality regardless. The set phrase yoroshiku onegaishimasu is useful when handing over your booking details.

keyPhrases.native: チェックインをお願いします
朝食は付いていますか
部屋を見せてください
チェックアウトは何時ですか
荷物を預かってください

grammar.explanation: To put a polite verb into the past, change -masu to -mashita. Shimasu (do) becomes shimashita (did), so yoyaku o shimashita means 'I made a reservation'. The negative past is -masen deshita.

examples.native: 予約をしました。(yoyaku o shimashita)

grammar.commonMistake: Saying shimasu deshita; the correct past of shimasu is shimashita, not masu plus deshita.

grammar.explanation: Onegaishimasu is a versatile polite request, slightly more formal than kudasai. Attach it after a noun to ask for a service or item: chekkuin o onegaishimasu ('check-in, please'). It carries a humble, courteous nuance ideal for service settings.

examples.native: 会計をお願いします。(kaikei o onegaishimasu)
タクシーをお願いします。(takushii o onegaishimasu)

grammar.commonMistake: Confusing kudasai (give me an object) with onegaishimasu (request a service); both are polite, but onegaishimasu suits services.

dialogue.setup: A guest arrives at a hotel front desk to check in.

lines.speaker: 客
lines.native: すみません、チェックインをお願いします。

lines.speaker: フロント
lines.native: ご予約はされましたか。

lines.speaker: 客
lines.native: はい、予約をしました。スミスです。
lines.transliteration: hai, yoyaku o shimashita. Sumisu desu
lines.english: Yes, I made a reservation. I'm Smith.

lines.speaker: フロント
lines.native: ありがとうございます。朝食は付いています。
lines.transliteration: arigatou gozaimasu. choushoku wa tsuite imasu

lines.speaker: 客
lines.native: チェックアウトは何時ですか。

lines.speaker: フロント
lines.native: 午前十一時です。こちらが鍵です。
lines.transliteration: gozen juuichiji desu. kochira ga kagi desu
lines.english: Eleven a.m. Here is your key.

lines.speaker: 客
lines.native: ありがとうございます。お世話になります。
lines.english: Thank you. I'll be in your care.

quiz.question: How do you say 'I made a reservation' in Japanese?
quiz.correctAnswer: 予約をしました (yoyaku o shimashita)
quiz.explanation: Shimashita is the polite past of suru (do), so this means 'I did a reservation'.

quiz.question: Which phrase asks 'Is breakfast included?'
options: 鍵をください (kagi o kudasai)
朝食は付いていますか (choushoku wa tsuite imasu ka)
一泊いくらですか (ippaku ikura desu ka)
部屋を見せてください (heya o misete kudasai)
quiz.correctAnswer: 朝食は付いていますか (choushoku wa tsuite imasu ka)
quiz.explanation: Tsuite imasu means 'is attached / included', so this asks about breakfast.

quiz.question: チェックインをお願い _____ 。(Check-in, please)
quiz.explanation: Onegaishimasu is the polite request phrase used for services.

quiz.question: What is a ryokan?
quiz.explanation: A ryokan is a traditional inn with tatami rooms, futon bedding, and often an onsen.

quiz.question: Arrange: ですか / 何時 / チェックアウトは (what time is check-out?)
quiz.correctAnswer: チェックアウトは何時ですか (chekkuauto wa nanji desu ka)
quiz.explanation: Topic + nanji desu ka asks 'at what time is [topic]?'.
